name: Fondant Easter Eggs
recipeIngredient: assorted food colorings (vanilla, almond, lemon, maple, or peppermint extract)
1 pound chocolate flavored candy coating or vanilla flavored candy coating
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat margarine and salt until fluffy.
2. Gradually beat in sweetened condensed milk, then 2 pounds powdered sugar until well blended.
3. Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions.
4. In medium bowls, tint each portion with a few drops of food coloring (desired flavoring: vanilla, almond, lemon, maple, or peppermint extract).
5. On a smooth surface, knead 1/4 to 1/2 cup powdered sugar into each portion until smooth.
6. Shape the mixture into 4 or 6 eggs.
7. Place the eggs on baking sheets; cover and chill until firm, about 4 hours or overnight.
8. Insert a fork into the bottom of each chilled egg.
9. Dip the eggs into warm candy coating; let excess coating drip off.
10. Place the eggs on wax paper-lined baking sheets.
11. Let them stand or chill until firm.
12. Decorate as desired with frosting.
13. Store covered at room temperature or in the refrigerator. If using vanilla-flavored candy coating, you may need to dip twice.
